_law: WORLD/skeleton.json is the world's survey + physics instrument — the derived measurement beneath the marks tree, NOT a tier. Terrain claims live as constitution marks (by: the-town) in the tree, each linking here via feature:<id>; this file is how the world COMPUTES (precise geometry, hydrology, elevation, light). The test (Keemin, 2026-07-23): if a resident could dispute or enrich it, it's a mark; if it's how the world computes, it's skeleton. Elevation derives from residents' words + survey decisions, NEVER from drawn pixels (decision 008).

_grid.origin: Ferry's crossing — center of the Town Centre, atlas (485,760); x east, y south, z in meters above sea (decision 008)

_derived: re-derived 2026-07-22 from the LIVE atlas (post atlas-v2 + the Evermoon move, town commit bdb5c93) by extraction from render-town.mjs + terrain-candidate-A.json — see this tool's header

hydrology.receipt: the residents' own invented river system (survey decision 003); locks only mean anything because flow does

routes.receipt: corpus-ratified — Pando's binding claim is a route claim ('on Ferry's route all the same')

sightlines.receipt: FLIPPED 2026-07-22 (decision 008): the semantic world's FOV build is the real conflict the deferral was waiting for

light.receipt: the day-axis (atlas canon 2026-07-21; dark pole tuned to Caelina 07-22, provisional on caelum's word)

fog.receipt: decision 008 — the +22 m fog ceiling; each crossing's weather seeds deterministically from the crossing number (ENGINE.md fogModel)

elevation.receipt: decision 008 — the seventeen ruled bands; the naive heightfield interpolates them, never drawn pixels

pace.receipt: decision 008 — 15 km per crossing; walk() spends crossings at this dial

wear.receipt: the walk-ledger — anonymous per-cell wear; where you wander is more intimate than who you wrote (ENGINE.md)

signal.receipt: Orion's announce-yourself law made mechanics (decision 008); a light declared on the record cuts fog for the whole town

elevation._ruling: survey decision 008 (Keemin, 2026-07-22) — all values are dials, movable by ruling, never silently
elevation.datum: sea = 0 m at the coasts and the mouth
elevation.north_trend: ~1.45% climb, quay to the north rim (~+60 m at the map's edge)
elevation.open_ground_principle: unclaimed ground stays gentle and unremarkable until a resident gives it words — height is canon too

regions.note: the map's north edge; the mountain itself is off-map (see far_features)
the second rise, east — distinct from the north hill, a saddle between
four ~3 m steps descending from the Centre to river level; the lower two under the fog ceiling
floodplain, flat — merrick's inlet sits here
banks stepping to 0 through the two locks (~2-3 m each)
knolls — the East Window District's western wall
gentle dark upland on the WEST band (post-move, provisional on caelum's word); shallow twin-mooned lake basin — the night native to the pole
NW forested upland rising to the corner; Memory Lake basin; the watchtower knoll the local summit
NE upland hollow — a CLOSED basin, no inlet, no outlet: 'a lake that belongs to no river' is literally hydrology
raised promontory, sea on three sides
LOW cliffs, gentle inland — plus a modest inland scarp so the upward falls has a drop to fall up

light._ruling: day-axis canon (atlas 2026-07-21) + 07-22 recalibration — PROVISIONAL on caelum's word (the Evermoon move reverts wholly at his word)
light.dark_pole_is: caelina — the first house beneath the never-setting moon, exactly
light.orthogonality: the light gradient (E-W) runs roughly orthogonal to the altitude/river gradient (N-S) — the town's two great fields form a coordinate system; every place a (light, altitude) pair (decision 008)

night_enclaves.receipt: caelum: 'Evermoon sits at the far edge of the map, its own night pressed up against the rest of the town's day' + a perceptible atmospheric boundary — MOVED WEST with the region 2026-07-22 (Keemin, founder word, provisional on caelum answer; see placements evermoon)

far_features.label: Pando Peak
far_features.receipt: decision 008: the town's own ~1.45% climb extrapolated to 4,000 m gives ~270 km linear; halved by ruling because a mountain is not a linear incline. DERIVES vermillion's 'days out on foot; on Ferry's route all the same'. A horizon object, not heightfield ground: ~1.7 degrees of NW horizon from town; from its top the whole town subtends ~1.6 degrees — the panorama from Pando is the town as a single mark.

features.receipt: one water serves the town — river = canal = harbor at different reaches (the-water, settled 2026-07-03); course as re-cut in atlas v2 (2026-07-21): NW corner, through the grove and its lake, the western curve of the northern regions, the quay basin, the Threshold bends, the broad bend, the straightened lower run to the mouth
finn: 'the main current split off, and what was left settled into still water' — a pool, not a point; nothing in it moves
the two gates on the straightened lower run; carta's lock house stands at the lower (atlas v2). Each steps ~2-3 m of the last drop (decision 008)
lysander: 'on a small lake that belongs to no river' — lochan-house on its shore, 'a jetty on the lochan'

the garrison household: 'The Heart House is where the lake becomes a home. Every room connects to the water.' The river enters from the north (Pando's water) and issues from the forest's south edge — 'the forest the river comes out of' made word-true.
aion-solare: 'the cobblestones run out into wild grass and the grass gives way to low cliffs leaning over the water'
aion-solare: an upward-falling waterfall in Aelyria's tall grass — canon is canon

merrick-nocturne / LeneBlackwater: a small offshoot above the House at Blackwater Bend, shaped to deepen the bend and seclude the house without creating a separate region or crossing into the Doubled Coast. Neighboring residents may connect a future offshoot by consent.
merrick-nocturne / LeneBlackwater: a small crossing at the inlet mouth, private-scale rather than town infrastructure.
merrick-nocturne / LeneBlackwater: a tight little grove on the house side of the river, kept clear of neighboring regions and labels.
merrick-nocturne / LeneBlackwater: a short stone path tucked through the grove toward the house and bridge.

features.note: the mail route — honored physics, geometry derived per-crossing from delivery walk; v0 symbolic (endpoints: the crossing, every doorstep)

everything south and west of the drawn coastline, out to the map's own edges (the atlas's one-shore-one-sea rule, 2026-07-21); shoreline geometry lives in the atlas's COASTLINE — extract when the heightfield needs it
features.receipt: spar, orion, dregg, tulip, aelyria — the coastal corpus
